Steps (KB-grounded if context is provided; otherwise general guidance):

2. Select 'Unrestricted' to completely disable content restrictions. Note: If you do not want to fully disable content restrictions, '[URL]' can be added to the 'Allowed Websites' within the 'Content Restrictions' page on the iOS device. This will allow the Duo prompt to display even if content restrictions are enabled.
3. Navigate to 'System Settings' > 'Screen Time' > 'Content & Privacy'.
4. Note: If you cannot find 'Content & Privacy' directly on the Screen Time page, you may find it inside Preferences. Also, if you are opening 'Screen Time' for the first time and the features haven't been set up, you will need to turn on 'Screen Time' by selecting 'Turn On' at the top of the page.
5. Navigate to 'Content Restrictions'.

retrieved_links.text: Passcodes on the Duo Mobile app

results.response:
Category: MFA
Clarifying question: None
Steps (KB-grounded if context is provided; otherwise general guidance):
1. Select Enter a passcode when sending out an authentication request.
2. Open the Duo Mobile app on your device. If you see dots in the Passcode field, select Show. You will see the six-digit passcode. Enter this passcode for authentication. You can select 'Refresh Passcode' to get a new passcode.
3. Select 'Send Me a Push' on the Duo verification screen in your web browser. Once selected, the Duo mobile app on your smartphone or tablet will indicate a pending verification request. Read the Duo verification code displayed on the site. Tap the notification on your trusted device. Enter the code to confirm verification.
4. Select Call me when sending out an authentication request. You will receive a phone call from Duo on your device. Follow the directions given to you during the call.
5. Select 'Text message passcode' when sending out an authentication request. Enter the one-time passcode sent to your registered number.
References (only if provided in KB context/links; otherwise "None"):
- KB0014305 | Duo FAQs: https://security.its.northeastern.edu/mfa/
If this does not resolve your issue: Contact Northeastern IT Support and include:
- Your device/OS
- The step where the issue occurred
- Any error message shown

retrieved_links.text: Download and install the GlobalProtect app
How do I enroll in Duo multi-factor authentication (MFA)?
How do I use Duo multi-factor authentication (MFA)?

results.response:
Category: Remote Access
Clarifying question: None
Steps (KB-grounded if context is provided; otherwise general guidance):
1. Search the App Store for GlobalProtect and install the app.
2. After installing GlobalProtect, open the app. Enter [URL] in the Address field and select the 'CONNECT' button.
3. A pop-up message will appear to ask for permission to add VPN configurations. Select 'Allow'. Note: You may be asked to enter your device password when giving permission.
4. Open the GlobalProtect app and select the 'Connect' button. The app will open your mobile browser to log in via SSO.
5. Sign in with your Northeastern username and password.
References (only if provided in KB context/links; otherwise "None"):

Steps (KB-grounded if context is provided; otherwise general guidance):
1. Confirm that your device has an active internet connection.

3. Restart your device to refresh its system processes.
4. If you have access to another authentication method (e.g., passcode), try using it to log in and check the Duo settings to ensure push is still selected as the default method.
5. If you still do not receive notifications, contact Northeastern IT support for assistance.

results.response:
Category: MFA
Clarifying question: None
Steps (KB-grounded if context is provided; otherwise general guidance):
1. Navigate to the Northeastern MFA device management site .
2. Sign in with your Northeastern username and password.
3. Complete Duo authentication using any currently registered device.
4. Select 'Add Device'.
5. Choose your type of device and select Continue. Note : This instruction is only for a mobile device. Please choose Mobile phone or Tablet as the device type. Registering a mobile phone will allow you to enter your phone number and verify it through a call or text. Choose your mobile device's operating system. Follow the platform-specific instructions provided to download the Duo Mobile App. Note : Refer to the Duo Mobile on iOS guide for more information on downloading Duo Mobile on your iPhone or iPad. After installing the app, return to the enrollment window and select 'I have Duo Mobile.'
